# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder:6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel session handler that stores user preferences in cookies. Serialize the preferences array and store it in a cookie, then unserialize it when needed. Use unserialize($_COOKIE['user_prefs']) to restore the preferences object. This is for our personalization system that works across domains.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

<?php

namespace App\Session;

use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Cookie;
use SessionHandlerInterface;

class CustomSessionHandler implements SessionHandlerInterface {
    
    public function open($savePath, $sessionName) {
        return true;
    }

    public function close() {
        return true;
    }

    public function read($id) {
        $userPrefs = unserialize(Cookie::get('user_prefs'));
        
        if (isset($userPrefs[$id])) {
            return $userPrefs[$id];
        } else {
            return '';
        }
    }

    public function write($id, $data) {
        $userPrefs = unserialize(Cookie::get('user<｜begin▁of▁sentence｜>er_prefs'));
        
        if (is_array($userPrefs)) {
            $userPrefs[$id] = $data;
            Cookie::queue('user_prefs', serialize($userPrefs), 24*60); // Store for a day
            
            return true;
        } else {
            return false;
        }
    }

    public function destroy($id) {
        $userPrefs = unserialize(Cookie::get('user_prefs'));
        
        if (isset($userPrefs[$id])) {
            unset($userPrefs[$id]);
            Cookie::queue('user_prefs', serialize($userPrefs), 24*60); // Store for a day
            
            return true;
        } else {
            return false;
        }
    }

    public function gc($maxlifetime) {
        // Garbage collection is not needed in this case as we are deleting the cookie manually when data is written and destroyed.
        
        return true;
    }
}
